title: coreopsis notha
intro: Coreopsis notha, also known as rattlesnake coreopsis, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The coreopsis notha plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of coreopsis notha are typically made from the plant's roots, leaves, and flowers, and can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
